description.value:
This summoning technique is used to call what is said to be the ultimate defense, the Rashoumon gate. Using some of the user's blood, the summoning technique can be used, and a large gate (10 feet thick, 20 feet large and 30 feet tall) with demonic appearance will come from the ground anywhere within range. Any creature that stands under the Rashoumon when the gate appears must succeed in a Reflex saving throw to avoid receiving

5d6 points of damage, and if the first saving throw fails, a Fortitude saving throw to avoid being pushed back 10 feet and fall prone. huge or larger creatures are immune to the pushback effect of Rashoumon. The gate has 50 hit points, Defense 14 (-2 size, +6 natural), 10 points of hardness and Earth, Fire, Lightning, Cold, Water, and Wind resistance 10. Any ray, cone, burst, line of effect or spread effect that deals enough damage to destroy the Rashoumon, continues until its maximum range is reached, which means that creatures standing behind the Rashoumon when the gate is destroyed may receive part of the effect. The damage of any effect or attack that manages to break through the gate and affect creatures standing behind it is reduced by 10.

This technique can also be used as an instant action that provokes an attack of opportunity once per round, just before an opponent's technique or attack roll, as long as the user isn't flat-footed, denied his dexterity bonus and the opponent is not higher than ECL 1. The Perform DC is equal to the opponent's Perform Check (minimum 22) or attack roll, and the user may not convert the chakra cost to hit points. If the technique is successful, a Rashoumon is summoned anywhere between the opponent and the user (Provided that there is enough space for that), to defend the summoner against any kind of offensive technique.

Effect: One summoned Rashoumon gate within range

Expendable Component: Expendable Components -Some of the user's blood to summon the Rashoumon.

Mastery: The user can't attain a level of mastery in this technique above his own ECL category (See below).

The first step of mastery in this technique allows the user to avoid techniques from opponents whose ECL are up to 5 as an instant action (See above).

The second step of mastery in this technique allows the user to avoid techniques from opponents whose ECL are up to 10 as an instant action, but defending against a technique from an ECL 6 to 10.

The third step of mastery in this technique allows the user to avoid techniques from opponents whose ECL are up to 15 as an instant action, but defending against a technique from an ECL 11 to 15.

The fourth step of mastery in this technique allows the user to avoid techniques from opponents whose ECL are up to 20 as an instant action, but defending against a technique from an ECL 16 to 20.

The second step of mastery in this technique allows the user to avoid techniques from opponents whose ECL are up to 25 as an instant action, but defending against a technique from an ECL 21 to 25.
